channel.title: The Open Stacks - recently added
channel.description: Newest additions to The Open Stacks, an anti-censorship library.

item.title: 1865-2001: Cuban Anarchism: The history of a movement - Frank Fernandez
item.description: Frank Fernandez's 2001 book Cuban Anarchism: The history of a movement, which gives a historical account of the rise and fall of Cuba's inspiring anarchist movement.

item.title: 1912: The Miners' Next Step
item.description: A document produced in 1912 by the South Wales Miners Federation, which was one of the high points of syndicalist organising in the UK.

item.title: 1939-1945: Anarchist activity in France during World War II
item.description: Patchy historical information about the activity of some anarchists - revolutionary and pacifist - in France during World War II and under occupation.

item.title: 1968-1971: The League of Revolutionary Black Workers - A. Muhammad Ahmad
item.description: A short history of the League of Revolutionary Black Workers - a radical union of black auto workers. The articles includes other information about the car industry, race and struggle from 1910 onwards.

item.title: 1982: The death of Neil Roberts - Class War Aotearoa
item.description: A short account of a little known political death in New Zealand. In 1982, an anarchist punk rocker attempted to blow up the Wanganui police computer. He blew himself up in the process.

item.title: 325 - Anti-Civilization Anarchist Journal
item.author: 325 Collective
item.description: An anarchist journal and distro focused on anti-civilization, insurrectionary anarchism, and anti-state resistance. 325 publishes communiques, analysis, and solidarity texts from anarchist and anti-authoritarian struggles worldwide. The site archives issues of its zine alongside a broad range of related texts and translations.

item.title: A 5-year plan for Sheffield IWW
item.description: Working in the tradition of Weakening the Dam (see Goals, Then Strategy, Then Tactics ) and shamelessly copying Lifelong Wobbly’s article, What’s Your Five Year Plan? , here’s an article that sets out a possible set of objectives for Sheffield IWW to hit by 1st January 2020.

item.title: A Brief Introduction To Ecological Anarchies - Julian Langer
item.author: Julian Langer
item.description: From the outset, it seems obvious that it would be impossible to reduce eco-anarchist praxes to what may be written and recorded for anyone to read, in the same way that it is impossible for any individual to visit a river and write down al

item.title: A collection of essays on feminism and sexism in the anarchist movement
item.description: Published by various authors on International Womens Day, 2011, to commemorate the international day of struggle for womens liberation.

item.title: A day mournful and overcast... An account of the Iron Column
item.category: Spanish Civil War and Revolution of 1936-9
item.description: A personal account of a liberated prisoner's experience as a fighter during the Spanish Civil War and Revolution of 1936-9 in the anarchist militia unit called the Iron Column prior to its incorporation into the Communist-run Republican army.

item.title: A Survey of the Anarchist Movement in the Ukraine: (1987–1994) - A. Dubovik & A. Skrozitsky
item.author: A. Dubovik & A. Skrozitsky
item.description: INTRODUCTION: This piece provides an insight into the encouraging growth of the anarchist movement in the Ukraine in the perestroika years and its decline soon afterwards as the USSR collapsed and the Ukraine became independent (late 1991).

item.title: A history of socialist thought - G.D.H. Cole
item.description: All of G.D.H. Cole's five volume (in seven parts) series chronicling the history of socialist thought from 1789 to 1939.

item.title: Between Analysis and Vision: Moving Beyond the Theory-Practice Dichotomy - A. Morefus
item.author: A. Morefus
item.description: I am cruising currently, right now. I am cruising because I have dedicated myself to all that is creative and destructive in my life, right now. And I am equally in love with every aspect of my life, and all the ingredients that have cause

item.title: A moving story: with afterword - Jocelyn Cohn and James Frey
item.description: In 2012, workers at a small moving company in New York City rose up against bad pay and dangerous work conditions. In the course of the struggle, much was revealed about how exploitation operates; how the enticements of 'self-expression' and a 'laid back' atmosphere serve to weaken consciousness and collective action. At the same time, this history reveals the opportunities and limits that workers

item.title: A New Anarchist FAQ
item.author: Raddle Collective
item.description: Introduction to this FAQ This FAQ was gradually developed as a wiki on raddle.me beginning in 2021, initially by collecting essays written for the wiki as early as 2018. It was created because many anarchists take seriou

item.title: A Pathless Journey - Julian Langer
item.author: Julian Langer
item.description: Introduction I am writing this following an invitation to write something on “punk” spirituality and theology. While I would never call myself a practitioner of “punk spirituality” or a “punk theologian”, I have been engaged in activities

item.title: A Tale of Nihilist Lovers - Anonymous
item.author: Anonymous
item.description: They couldn't catch us. Madison, Wisconsin. Do you remember the feeling of being consumed by maniacal laughter after our first intercourse with illegality? Nah, it won't change the world and most wouldn't think much of it. And it certainly

item.title: Coalitions, Libertarian Communism and Ireland - A. W. Zurbrugg
item.author: A. W. Zurbrugg
item.description: How did British Libertarian Communists understand the conflict in the north of Ireland, and face up to events there?1 Did they support specific campaigns for withdrawing British troops out of Ireland? Firstly a few words on organisations wi

item.title: Blackness and Democratic Modernity - A.X.
item.author: A.X.
item.description: “Modernity as a concept means time, the present. There are different moderns, depending on the age. From Sumerian modernity to Roman modernity, and even before and after them, there have been…many examples of modernity.” Abdullah Öcalan, T

item.title: Anarchism, History and Movement in the Philippines - a3yo
item.author: a3yo
item.description: In March 2012, we had the chance to meet some anarchists from the local infoshop and mindsetbreakerpress in manila, the capital of philippines. We talked to them about their general situation and about one campaign against gentrification an

item.title: An Anti-Civilization Mythology: A Review of Lierre Keith’s “The Vegetarian Myth” - Abbey Volcano
item.author: Abbey Volcano
item.description: I understand completely why someone might want to write a book about the “myths” of vegetarianism. We live in a world where capitalism has this amazing ability to co-opt anything and everything (you’ve almost got to admire what a good job c

item.title: Durruti in the Spanish Revolution
item.author: Abel Paz
item.description: TO JENNY, WHOSE CONSTANT AND CONTINUED SUPPORT MADE THIS BOOK POSSIBLE. TRANSLATOR\u2019S ACKNOWLEDGEMENTS I translated this book in honor of Durruti\u2019s revolutionary legacy and, to a lesser extent, Paz\u2019s contributions as a pa

item.title: Anarchist Economics: An alternative for a world in crisis - Abraham Guillen
item.author: Abraham Guillen
item.description: INTRODUCTION Spain has a particular historic significance for the world anarchist movement. The extent and breadth of publications on the Spanish Civil War and the Revolution of 1936-9 is ever expanding. This is wholly understandable since

item.title: Anarchism and Education - Abraham P. DeLeon
item.author: Abraham P. DeLeon
item.description: Although many activists have embraced anarchist theory, anarchism has been present in a variety of different academic areas as well. Anarchist sociology has been argued for (Purkis 2004), as well as the beginning sketches of an anarchist an

item.title: Active Distribution
item.author: Active Distribution Collective
item.description: A UK-based anarchist and punk distro that has been distributing zines, books, and records since the 1990s. Active Distribution stocks a wide range of anarchist, anti-fascist, animal liberation, and DIY punk literature. It is a key hub for the intersection of anarchist politics and underground music culture in Britain and Europe.

item.title: Beyond Hope - Adam Greenfield
item.author: Adam Greenfield
item.category: Ill Will Editions; mutual aid; climate change; long emergency;
item.description: As the real impacts of climate change wreak ever greater havoc on our lives, it is increasingly clear that “climate change” has also become an ideological apparatus meant to entrap us in despair, policy paralysis, and reformist horizons. Wh

item.title: Building a Revolutionary Movement: Why Anarcho-Communist Organisation - Adam Weaver
item.author: Adam Weaver
item.description: Over the past few years Anarchist-Communist organizations have been budding across the globe from South Africa to South America to North America. Yet few people, even within anarchist and revolutionary circles, have a good grasp of the beli

item.title: Collectivism: First article - Adhémar Schwitzguébel
item.author: Adhémar Schwitzguébel
item.description: Socialism first presented itself to the laboring classes in the form of different systems, each having its more or less numerous adepts, and each presenting itself as the infallible Gospel which must save society. These different socialist

item.title: An Anarchist and Filipino Diaspora Perspective on the COVID-19 Pandemic - Adobong Anarkiya
item.author: Adobong Anarkiya
item.description: It has been 2–3 months since the COVID-19 virus had spread across the world infecting hundreds of thousands and killing tens of thousands, leading the World Health Organization (WHO) to declare it a pandemic. Within that span of time, place

item.title: Anarchism isn’t a fantasy and it’s not a political theory: It’s a collective name for whatever forms of society can exist without murder as a political tool - Adrian Riskin
item.author: Adrian Riskin
item.description: When people say anarchism is a fantasy I believe they’re thinking of the apparent impossibility of setting up an anarchist government, imposing it on people, voting it into place, getting everyone to agree to its terms. They’re thinking of

item.title: Book review: Jesse COHN, “Underground Passages. Anarchist resistance culture, 1848-2011” - Adrian Tătăran
item.author: Adrian Tătăran
item.description: Anarchism seems to have attracted a steady growing interest since the late nineties. The number and quality of studies since, dedicated to the various facets of a movement previously and for a long time relegated to oblivion or derision, be

item.title: AFed statement on kneecapping of nuclear executive by Informal Anarchist Federation - Anarchist Federation
item.author: Anarchist Federation
item.description: On the 11th of May Roberto Adinolfi, CEO of an Italian state controlled nuclear engineering company, was shot and wounded. A cell of the insurrectionist Informal Anarchist Federation have claimed responsibility for the attack in a statement

item.title: Anti-Capitalism, Mutual Aid, and Asset-Based Community Development - Afrofuturist Abolitionists of the Americas
item.author: Afrofuturist Abolitionists of the Americas
item.description: “We say we’re not going to fight capitalism with black capitalism, but we’re going to fight it with socialism”. -Fred Hampton LIFE UNDER CAPITALISM Life under Capitalism is not just the “normal” state of affairs or the result of “human nat

item.title: Christian Supercessionism: A Problem for the Black Movement - Afrofuturist Abolitionists of the Americas
item.author: Afrofuturist Abolitionists of the Americas
item.description: Conversations about anti-Jewishness in Black Movements are hard to have when we consider the reality that Zionism is real. This Kickback will be a very quick flash through of many historical concepts: anti-Semitism in European Christianity,

item.title: Anarchist Communism in its Relation to State Socialism - Agnes Henry
item.author: Agnes Henry
item.category: Liberty: A Journal of Anarchist Communism
item.description: The question of how for Anarchist Communism agrees exactly with State Socialism, and the exact line that divides them, has long seemed to me one that it would be well to enter into. And just now, in view of the approaching International Con

item.title: The CNT defense committees in Barcelona 1933–1938: An interview with Agustín Guillamón - Agustin Guillamón
item.author: Agustin Guillamón
item.description: On the occasion of the 75th anniversary of the Spanish Revolution, our friend and collaborator Agustín Guillamón was interviewed by the editors of the website alasbarricadas.org about his latest book, Los Comités de Defensa de la CNT en Bar

item.title: AHALAN: Anti-Humanism, Anti-Life, Anti-Natalism. A philosophical essay on the ethics of existence - Simanski
item.author: Simanski
item.description: Preface This text began as a conversation between four people who will probably never finish anything together again. That is not a tragedy; it is simply what happens. People move, disperse, get absorbed into the machinery of survival. The

item.title: Antinomians and Nonconformists - Ahmet Karamustafa
item.author: Ahmet Karamustafa
item.description: The early Sufis of the third/ninth century occupied a peculiar place in the social and mental world of Islamic Iraq. Unlike many itinerant renunciants who roamed the countryside, the Sufis firmly implanted themselves into the major urban ce

item.title: Ainriail / Raveiw magazine
item.author: Outta Control
item.description: Complete online archive of Ainriail, a magazine published by Belfast Anarchist collective between 1985 and 1987.

item.title: Against Decentralism - Ajesiroo
item.author: Ajesiroo
item.description: The increasing ubiquity of distributed networks represents a huge leap in progress, but we have to actually make use of them. Decentralism in the setting of this essay is referring to the tendency to narrowly focus on dispersing the concent

item.title: Anarchy is deeply ideological, and that’s okay - Ajesiroo
item.author: Ajesiroo
item.description: As a non-hierarchical form of social organisation, anarchy must be paired with socialism. This necessitates that basic needs such as housing and healthcare are provided on an unconditional basis. When we’re conscious of the prerequisite for

item.title: Al Amal Newsletter
item.description: Al Amal (Hope) is a new bimonthly newsletter produced by the Sudanese Anarchist Gathering, documenting issues and struggles of Sudanese Anarchists and conditions for Sudanese people.

item.title: Anarchist-Communism - Alain Pengam
item.author: Alain Pengam
item.description: Introduction Anarchist-communism has been regarded by other anarchist currents as a poor and despised relation, an ideological trophy to be exhibited according to the needs of hagiography or polemic before moving on to “serious things” (th

item.title: Anarchism and Agriculture - Alan Albon
item.author: Alan Albon
item.description: <verse For the Father of Agriculture Gave us a hard calling: he first decreed it an art To work the fields, sent worries to sharpen our mortal wits And would not allow his realm to grow listless from lethargy. Before Jove’s time no settlers

item.title: Anarchists in the Chilean Labour Movement - Alan Angell
item.author: Alan Angell
item.description: There were three early working-class political organizations in Chile: the Democratic party, the anarchists, and the socialist movements which gave rise to the Socialist Workers’ Party (POS), which later became the Communist Party (CP). The

item.title: Analytical Anarchism: Some Conceptual Foundations - Alan Carter
item.author: Alan Carter
item.description: ; ANALYTICAL ANARCHISM Some Conceptual Foundations ; ALAN CARTER University of London In the 1980s, Marxist political philosophy suffered mixed fortunes. On one hand, it underwent a considerable demise in Eastern Europe as a state- promoted

item.title: Anarchism: some theoretical foundations - Alan Carter
item.author: Alan Carter
item.description: Abstract This article considers two different, yet related, theoretical approaches that could be employed to ground the anarchist critique of Marxist- Leninist revolutionary practice, and thus of the state in general: the State-Primacy The

item.title: Beyond primacy: Marxism, anarchism and radical green political theory - Alan Carter
item.author: Alan Carter
item.description: Abstract The most sophisticated philosophical defence of Marx’s theory of history– G.A. Cohen’s—deploys functional explanations in a manner that accords explanatory primacy to technological development. In contrast, an anarchist theory can

item.title: Anarchism in Indonesia: A 2010 interview with Indonesian anarchists about the anarchist movement there. - Anonymous
item.author: Anonymous
item.description: Can you tell us about the history of anarchism in Indonesia? MT: As far as I know from my friends' stories and from what I’ve learned, the origin of anarchism in Indonesia came together with the arrival of punk music around 1998. At that ti

item.title: Anarchism and a moneyless economy - Alan MacSimóin
item.author: Alan MacSimóin
item.description: Anarchists are usually pretty good at listing the things we are against: capitalism, racism, religious sectarianism, authoritarianism and so on. We are usually pretty good at explaining how best to struggle: direct democracy and mass direct
